meaning: adj.(～ to) 由于,该付的,未付的,应归功于
examples: Owing to staff shortages, there was no food on the plane.
Owing to circumstances beyond our control , the flight to Rome has been cancelled.
Owing to bad weather, the plane had to change its course.
However, expenses grew from 2005 to 2009 owing heavy marketing and promotional activities to generate sales.
The number of consumer purchases reduced in 2008 and 2009, owing to the weak economy.
